name: Global Men's Meditation
notes:
This a meditation meeting which has a different form of meditation every day of the week. 
It meets from 8:30am to 9:30am PST, 11:30am EST

Public Email Contact: kkcbmeetings@gmail.com

name: How It Works Las Vegas Thursday Book Study Open
Queer and Anonymous
notes: Meditation and Participation - 15 minute meditation followed by open sharing. Open Meeting, LGBTQIA focused.

name: Conscious Contact Online
notes:
Conscious Contact Online meets on Sundays at 8PM EST. The first 40 minutes are open discussion and the last 20 minutes are spent in guided meditation. No special knowledge of meditation is needed and we are theologically agnostic. We deeply embrace the concepts of a loving Higher Power, a respect for each persons journey and believe that the Steps are not something we do so much as something that we become. Our meetings are informal and all are welcome to attend!

We are the online Sister group of the in-person Conscious Contact group which meets in York, PA on Wednesdays at 8PM.

name: 24hr International Marathon Meeting
notes:
A popular safe meeting for Newcomers and Old Timers alike.
We have become a family here and are grounded in the Steps & Traditions of Alcoholics Anonymous.
If you want to get sober, you can stay with us 24 hours a day if needed.
If you cannot sleep, stay with us, if you’re feeling tempted, talk with us If you just want to listen, listen.
We share our Experience Strength and Hope with you To help you beat this Cunning, Baffling & Powerful disease.

name: 4th Dimensioners 9 PM Eastern Online AA Meeting
notes:
Log In Early and Stay Late for the Meeting Before and After the Meeting!

Meeting Verification Provided for Attendees of Every Meeting!

Meeting Format: Nightly Discussion of the Topic of the Chairperson’s
Choosing and a Speaker Every Saturday!

Must Log Into a Free Zoom.us Account to Access

name: Woodland Online Group Daily Reflections Tues. & Living Sober Thurs.
notes:
Meetings Tuesdays and Thursdays at 9:00 AM PST -  open early for fellowship.
Every Tuesday - Daily Reflections/discussion
Every Thursday - Living Sober book study/discussion


Public Email : Woodlandonlinegroup@gmail.com

name: Recovery in da House
notes: Zoom meeting, speaker/discussion group with a reading from the Big Book or the 12X12.  Meets at 9pm Hawaii Standard Time, 7 days a week for one hour.

name: Wake Up World Wide
notes: Our format includes a number of AA daily readings as well as a few pages in the Big Book. We typically allow for 30 mins of group sharing.

name: On Awakening
notes:
11th step format AA meeting with a 15 min meditation, daily reflections and sharing.
Public Email : step11pdx@gmail.com

name: VEGAS Deliver us From Ego
notes:
Zoom on in to VEGAS for our nightly 10PM (pacific) meeting Deliver Us From Ego.  
Lively topic discussions with a different flair every night from our revolving selection of hosts.

name: Rise and Shine
notes:
Daily Reflections Meeting 
No password required - waiting room enabled.

name: A Vision for You Pacific
notes:
A Vision for You is a meeting of Alcoholics Anonymous that meets 5pm Pacific Time 5 nights a week Monday through Friday. Our primary purpose is to carry the message of Alcoholics Anonymous and practice the principles of the 12 steps in all our affairs. Our group is guided by the 12 traditions of AA as outlined in the 12 steps and 12 traditions. A Vision for You has no affiliation with any outside enterprises or denominational affiliations nor opposes nor endorses any other cause our primary purpose is to stay sober and help other alcoholics achieve sobriety according to the same principles of the founders of AA.

Meeting has a waiting room

name: Serenity Now Group
notes:
The Serenity Now group of Alcoholics Anonymous is an online only. All are welcome. We ask that sharing your experience, strength and hope be limited only to those who seek to achieve or maintain sobriety. 

This group is LGBTQ+ friendly and no alcoholic will be turned away. We ask that you attend this zoom meeting as you would present yourself at an in person meeting. This meeting is to be a safe place for everyone. We meet seven days a week at 7pm EST. 

A valid Zoom account is required to access this meeting.

name: WGAT Virtual - Discussion
notes:
This one-hour meeting is an open discussion meeting of Alcoholics Anonymous where the topic is always "What's Good About Today."

What's Good About Today Virtual (WGAT Virtual) meets morning and evening, seven days a week, and is hosted by a variety of group members from all over the United States & Canada.

We provide time for sharing any burning desires and support for newcomers. Come join our family of unconditional love. We hope to see you soon!

Visit our website for additional information.

name: AA Alternativo
notes: Una reunion de aplicacion zoom diaria que se centra en leer y discutir temas de los Libros de ALcoholicos Anonimos. Reunion abiertas a todos que buscan informacion y quienes buscab ayuda referente a su problema con el alcohol... Se reune todos los dias a las 8:30 pm horario de California..

name: One Day at a Time in CNY Woman's Meeting
notes:
Eastern Standard time Lunch Hour closed discussion meeting for woman.  
Let us take a few minutes out of our day to have our vitamin AA and give each other life perspective while we share our experience strength and hope.  
This meeting is closed discussion with daily topics.

name: Shivering Denizens Daily Beginners
notes:
A daily Beginners meeting focusing on offering newcomers practical REAL help in getting through the tricky early days. 
Each day a guest speaker discusses topics taken from Living Sober. 

Count your days with the Denizens.
Everyone gets a warm welcome.

Every afternoon at 4:30 PM GMT

name: Eye Openers Online
notes:
Open AA meeting established March 2020 with daily meetings originating from New Orleans at 7:15 am CST. 
Attendance verification is provided upon request.

Daily Meetings include:
Tuesday, Wednesday and Sunday are AA Literature meetings.
Monday, Thursday, Friday and Saturday are speaker discussion meetings or moderator's choice, usually emphasizing the principles of the program or other AA tools.

All are Invited to attend, listeners are welcomed, and sharing is voluntary. 
We welcome all who wish to join us as we carry the message of recovery to fellow alcoholics.

name: Happy Hour Red Deer
notes: Step & Topic Group 5:15 MST

name: Sunday Morning Reading Room
notes:
Sunday Morning Reading Room is a closed meeting of Alcoholics Anonymous. 
Each week we read and discuss a specific selection from AA approved literature. 
We believe active participation and honest sharing will enhance our understanding of the program of Alcoholics Anonymous, and lead to personal growth.

name: Hero AA Zen Zone
notes: We close the night with "On Retiring" from page 86 of the beautiful Big Book and we get in the Zen zone with music and guided meditation! Great way to close the night :)

name: On the Grid AA Meeting
notes:
We are a Home Group that meets every night at 9PM EST. Every evening follows a different format.
Sunday Step Study
Monday Topic/ Speaker
Tuesday Daily Reading
Wednesday Beginners Meeting
Thursday Speaker Series

name: AA Opportunity Speaker Series
notes:
This one-hour meeting is very newcomer friendly, especially those needing attendance verification. Voluntary sharing only at this meeting, no one will be called upon to share. 
Cameras are not required but must be on to be unmuted. If you would like your camera on at any time during the meeting, please turn it on during the pre-meeting before the speaker begins. No new videos will be allowed on once the meeting begins. 
Everyone is welcome to attend. Attendance verification given on request . Every meeting is a speaker meeting with recommended speakers from around the world. 
We meet every Thursday, Friday, Saturday and Sunday at 5:30 pm ET, 4:30 pm CT, 3:30 pm MT, 2:30 pm pacific, 1:30 pm AST, and 11:30 am HST 
International times: 9:30 pm GMT, 10:30 pm BST, 11:30 pm CEST, 12:30 am EEST, 7:30 am AEST

name: Morning Meetings
notes:
Morning Meetings meets Monday through Friday from 830am - 930am EST over Zoom. 

Our meeting is a closed meeting of Alcoholics Anonymous. Attendance at closed meetings is limited to those who have a desire to stop drinking.

Our meeting format changes daily:

Monday “A Vision for You” - Topic Meeting with Speaker 
Tuesday “Quarantine Promises” - Speaker Meeting
Wednesday “There is a Solution” - Big Book Meeting with Speaker 
Thursday  “Quarantine Promises” - Speaker Meeting
Friday “There is a Solution” - Step Meeting with Speaker

name: Get Sober Win Prizes
notes: In our meeting, we read AA literature except for the last Monday. Literature  can be AA  pamphlets, grapevines, living sober, etc.  The last Monday we celebrate sobriety milestones with a speaker meeting, and you can “take a chip” presented by a loved one . We celebrate 24 hrs, 30/60/90 days, 6/9 months every week. Our focus is on a comprehensive intro to AA and having fun! Come get a chip and a PRIZE! (will be mailed),  Closed caption.

name: Renfrew International 12 and 12
notes:
Renfrew International 12 and 12 is a Scottish-based meeting with an international collective of attendees. 
The group reads from the Twelve Steps and Twelve Traditions six nights a week Monday-Saturday and concludes the week with a speaker meeting on Sundays. 
Our meeting has been running since the 23rd of March 2020. So wherever you are in the world please join us for fellowship. 
For safety, please enable your video and use a real name when entering the meeting. 
No password required.

name: On Awakening Group
notes:
On Awakening (OA) is an open Alcoholics Anonymous (AA) meeting. We are an opening and friendly bunch that welcomes beginners, long-timers, and everyone in-between especially newcomers!!! OA meets every day using the tools of the AA program to get and stay sober. We have a different format each day with our group conscience meeting the first Friday of the month after the meeting. 

Sunday: As Bill Sees It
Monday: Daily Reflections 
Tuesday: Open Discussion 
Wednesday: Big Book 
Thursday: Living Sober 
Friday: Steps & Traditions 
Saturday: Step11/Meditation 

Once you have entered the meeting please press *6 to mute/share, *9 to raise your hand.

Don’t be shy and come on in!

name: AA After Hours
notes:
A daily online support  Zoom meeting with readings & open discussion from The Daily Reflection, AA Big Book, & more. We are not a glum lot. Grounded in the Steps & Traditions of AA, we share our Experience Strength & Hope with Newcomers & Old Timers alike.

Tradition #3 in Alcoholics Anonymous states, "The only requirement for membership is the desire to stop drinking." Open to all; everyone is welcome to attend. Meets daily from 10 pm to 12 am USA EST. If you want to get sober or if you’re feeling tempted, talk with us. If you just want to listen in, come on in.

name: East meets West As Bill Sees It
Sober Sunrise
notes:
Open meeting on Zoom, 5 days per week Monday through Friday at 6:45 MST. Everyone is welcome.
Please turn your video on briefly in order to be admitted to the meeting.
Monday - Stories from the Big Book 
Tuesday - 12 & 12 Step Study 
Wednesday - Big Book Study 
Thursday - Daily Reflections 
Friday - Traditions, Speaker, History, Grapevine, or As Bill Sees It

name: Women's HOW Online
notes:
Women's Closed Step Study Discussion (LGBT FRIENDLY). Join us as we read the 12 Steps and 12 traditions and The Little Red Book for Women.
Saturdays, 6:30 am PST.

This is a closed meeting of Alcoholics Anonymous. Attendance is limited to those with a desire to stop drinking.

If you’re new to AA and think you have a problem with alcohol, welcome! You’re in the right place.

name: SPIRIT OF THE UNIVERSE A.A.
notes:
Spirit of the Universe A.A. 
Join our warm and compassionate community dedicated to recovery. We welcome everyone with open arms.
Discover a variety of meetings, including Big Book readings, Speaker sessions, Traditions discussions, as well as Men's and Women's meetings, and more. Visit our website for meeting schedules.
Become a part of our recovery family. We follow the Big Book, work with sponsors, embrace the 12 steps and 12 traditions, and carry the spirit of altruism in our hearts to help fellow Alcoholics take back their lives with serenity.
With love,
The Spirit AA Team
SPIRIT OF THE UNIVERSE
Daily 9:00 AM meeting: Prayer and Meditation Meeting, open to alcoholics and non-alcoholics. All Higher Powers are welcome. 
Saturday 6:30 PM meeting: Open AA meeting. 
All other listed meetings are closed AA meetings. E-mail verification is provided at all meetings. 
*** 
1X a Week:
WOMENS- MON 6:30PM
MENS- THURS 6:30PM

name: It Works It Really Does 6pm Men's Open AA Meeting
notes: A daily Men's Meeting of Alcoholics Anonymous, Format is Chairpersons choice including Big Book readings, Speaker, Steps, etc... Open to all Men. Meets from 6pm-7pm  EST daily, 7 days a week duration.

name: A.A. GRUPO SOARES MORENO
notes:
Visão geral da reunião: por exemplo. "Uma reunião POR VIDEOCONFERÊNCIA que se concentra na leitura de Livros de A.A., de capa a capa. Aberta a todos; todos (as) são bem-vindos (as).
Para ver mais números de telefone, clique neste link: https://meet.google.com/nas-kgzx-met

name: We Need Each Other
notes: 7:30pm, meets weekdays. Each night brings a new host and topic or meeting style, open to all and everyone is welcome to attend! :)

name: Worldwide Primary Purpose Beginners Meeting
notes:
Once a beginner, always a beginner. If you have a desire to stop drinking, please join us as we share our experience, strength, and hope on the first three steps of the Big Book and reach out to the newcomers. 5:00 p.m. CST 7 days a week.

Closed Meeting
Waiting Room enabled

name: Worldwide Primary Purpose Daily Reflections
notes:
Come join us! We have someone read the "Daily Reflections" and give a short lead. We then breakout into smaller
groups to allow everyone the opportunity to share. 
We meet 7 days a week.
Court Verification Available
Closed Meeting

name: Worldwide Primary Purpose Gratitude Meeting
notes:
Join our Daily Gratitude Meeting! We take turns giving a short lead on what we are grateful for, followed by discussion.
Come and share what you are grateful for on a daily basis.
(closed meeting)

No court verification signatures.

name: Unidad sin Fronteras
notes:

name: Puerto Rico Sobriety Network
notes:
We are  the Puerto Rico Sobriety Network offering English AA meetings in San Juan, Puerto Rico. 
Our morning meetings are based on the Daily Reflections. 
Evening meetings are as follows: 
Monday | Format: Step and Tradition
Tuesday | Format: Topic Discussion 
Wednesday | Format: As Bill Sees It 
Thursday | Format: Big Book 
Friday | Format: Beginners 
Saturday | Format: Grapevine 
Sunday | Format: Reflections on AA Literature

name: Warsaw English Speaking Meeting of AA
AA Zoo Crew22 - The Morning Zoo
AA Zoo Crew22 - Prayer & Meditation Made Simple
AA Zoo Crew22 - The No Matter What Club
Beggars, Tramps & Queers
notes:
Beggars, Tramps & Queers is an open meeting of Alcoholics Anonymous that meets 7 days a week at 8:30 pm ET. 
We welcome anyone with a desire to stop drinking, including LGBTQIA+ and allies. 
And, we take care to respect diverse opinions about the practice and interpretation of AA principles. All our meetings allow time for discussion.

Sunday – Came to Believe
Monday – Glad Day Affirmations
Tuesday – Soundtrack for Sobriety
Wednesday – Wheel of Sobriety
Thursday – Literature for Recovery
Friday – Agnostic Sobriety
Saturday – Newcomer Meeting

name: AA Zoo Crew22 - A Fresh Start Beginner's Meeting
Virtual Sober Start
notes:
Virtual Sober Start is an online platform literature base A.A. group.
We meet seven days a week at 6:30am Eastern Standard Time on zoom with access through our website as well as via the blue button provided. 
Please visit our website for free resources, including pdf versions of the Big Book, 12x12, Living Sober and other AA-approved literature and AA audio speaker recordings.

name: Everynighters Online
notes: Big Book Discussion on the reading covered out of the first 164 pgs. including roman numerals. The room usually opens 30 mins prior to meeting time. Randomized list of shares made until the start of the meeting. 3 minute shares. Burning desire before names are called.

name: Choose Wisely Campaign
notes:
Our group is located in Minot ND. We are 12 Step and or Big Book Driven. We choose a different topic everyday. Our objective is to carry the AA message to the alcoholic who still suffers. We provide a safe place for newcomers and long timers to share their experience, strength and hope. We are not a glum lot. Our Meeting Schedule is 7 days a week. All times are Central Times. 
Sunday (Big Book Study) 2:00pm Central
Monday through Friday 12pm Central
Saturday at 10:00am Central

name: Morning Hope
notes:
A daily meeting - no passcode required.

Monday: Daily Reflection

Tuesday: Steps/Traditions

Wednesday: Chairpersons Choice AA Approved Literature

Thursday: Daily Reflection

Friday: Speaker Meeting

Saturday: Big Book

Sunday: Open Discussion Topic (AA APPROVED)

name: Sunrise Group
notes:
This is an open meeting of Alcoholics Anonymous which is all-inclusive and attended by people from all over the world. We welcome women, men, members of the LGBTQIA++ community, newcomers and long timers alike. We use a different AA meeting format for every day of the week using AA approved literature only, including topic discussion, Big Book study, speaker meeting, 12 and 12 study, and Big Book story read.
Meets at 7:00am ET daily, duration is 45 minutes.

This meeting is Waiting Room enabled. It is locked at 7:00 am, so please join us by 6:50am by using the first name/last Initial format to identify yourself on your Zoom account and use your live video to introduce yourself (you can turn it off later).

name: Agnostics In Progress
notes:
Agnostics in Progress meets Tuesdays at noon PST.  
Anyone with a desire to stop drinking is welcome, believer or not! 

We share our stories and the ways we have made progress in the program.  In this meeting you are free to speak your truth.

Our only wish is to assure you that anyone may achieve recovery in AA without having to deny their own beliefs.

We are here to lift each other up and find strength in the fellowship.  We seek progress, not perfection.

name: Україно - та російськомовна група з України
On Awakening
notes: On Awakening is a daily, closed women's 11th step meeting with a focus on meditation and journaling. The format of this meeting is that we read a few brief excerpts related to Step 11 from the Big Book and 12 & 12, then have eleven minutes of silence to meditate & journal. After the period of mediation, people may share their journal entries and/or thoughts as they relate to the 11th step or recovery from alcoholism.

name: AA Atlas
notes:
All who desire to get sober are welcome - new, old, and returning. Meets daily.
Group will include -daily shares, reflections, big book readings, 12 Steps and Traditions, and more concepts to help you map out your design for living.

name: The NYC Workshop 7:15AM Meeting
notes:
A virtual meeting focusing on Literature topics.
No password required.  Waiting room enabled.

name: AA Serenity Hour Virtual
AA Visionaries
notes:
AA Visionaries Meets Every Day, (7 Days a Week) at 7pm PST/ 9pm CST/ 10pm EST.

*Come and share your vision of experience, strength and hope with this casual crew! Visionaries is an Open Meeting, Welcome to all lengths of sobriety from newcomers to old timers…We have a Tradition of The Month and super cool new format for every day of the week. 
EACH NIGHT WE MEET TO GAIN A BETTER UNDERSTANDING OF ONLY AA APPROVED LITERATURE.

Monday - Tradition Night (12 & 12 or Illustrated Traditions)
• Tuesday- Big Book Study - 1st 164 Pages
• Wednesday- Living Sober
• Thursday- Stories from the Back of the Big Book
• Friday - STEP Night (12&12)
• Saturday- GAME NIGHT! Daily Reflections Spin Wheel
• Sunday- Illustrated Concepts

***After the meeting we do have a parking lot for fellowship. Our Group Conscience meets the First Friday of Every Month after the meeting . Come Share your Vision of What Sobriety is like for you!

name: TGIF Thank God I'm Free
notes:
Mondays-Fridays at NOON Eastern Time
Mon: BB Study
Tue: Speaker/Discussion
Wed: Step Study (12&12)
Thu: Beginners
Fri: Daily Reflections
Public Email : debra.timothy@gmail.com

name: AA Acceptance and Gratitude
notes:
All Welcome to Attend an Open Discussion Meeting. All Traditions followed and Anonymity is the Spiritual Foundation of all our Traditions. 
This will be an English Speaking Meeting. Please stop by a few minutes early to chat. 

If Password is enabled, please use email to get it.

Sunday - Thursday:  8am Eastern -- Open Discussion
Friday: 8am Eastern -- Beginners Meeting
Saturday: 8am Eastern -- Open Discussion

name: Early Risers
notes:
This group meets virtually, via Zoom conference, Monday - Friday at 7:00 AM, and at 9:00 AM on Saturday. Eastern Time
Our meetings are as follows:
Monday - Daily Reflections
Tuesday - Living Sober
Wednesday - Big Book (On the last Wednesday of the month, we will read from the Personal Stories section) 
Thursday - Open Topic 
Friday - Step Meeting (On the First Friday of the month we will read that month’s Tradition) 
Saturday - Speaker Meeting

All are welcome!

name: Kerman AA Meeting
notes:
The kerman AA online meeting is held on: Saturdays, Mondays, wednesdays and fridays in Iran from 5 PM to 6 PM (dates and times may change in the future). Every men and women and all genders and races can participate from all around the world provided that they speak persian ( Farsi ). You can join the following free-conference link below to join us

به ما بپیوندید در گروه الکلی های گمنام  آنلاین کرمان به وقت ایران تحت نرم افزار فری کانفرنس، لینک زیر،  چهار روز در هفته یعنی روزهای شنبه، دوشنبه، چهارشنبه و جمعه از ساعت ۱۷ الی ۱۸

name: Never Alone Again
notes:
The Never Alone Again is a one hour Speaker/Discussion meeting open to anyone with the desire to stop drinking.  Originally born out of downtown Boston meetings, we now welcome members from all across the world. The virtual discussion room is open 15 minutes before and after the meeting for fellowship.


Please visit our website for details about the meeting, links to other meetings and to sign up as a speaker (90 days sobriety requested). We would love to hear from you.

name: Rebellion Dogs
notes:
Rebellion Dogs is a daily closed men's AA meeting limited to those who have a desire to stop drinking.  The group holds a closed literature meeting on Zoom every day from 12 noon to 1 PM Eastern time. We open each meeting with a reading from conference-approved literature.  

To join the meeting please click on the blue zoom button or go to the meeting website by clicking on the website button e or email us for the link by clicking on the email button - rebelliondogs7@gmail.com

name: Woodland Online Group
notes:
Daily 6:30 AM PST: 
Meditation (Sun)
As Bill Sees It (M, W, F)
Big Book (Tue, Sat) 
12/12 (Thurs)

name: A Check-Up from the Neck UP (Get Your Mind Right)
notes:
Our Beginner's Literature and Step Meeting is 7 days a week at 5 pm EST. Come jumpstart your recovery and get your mind right.

The Zoo Crew has been on Zoom since March of 2020.  When the world shut down during the pandemic, we opened our Virtual Doors!

We are going on a Sober Cruise in May of 2023. Join us.

Alcoholism centers in the mind. It is cunning, baffling and powerful and unless the obsession to drink is removed we are defenseless against that first sucker drink. (BB pg. 37) “Our behavior is as absurd and incomprehensible with respect to the first drink as that of an individual with a passion, say, for jay-walking.” Come get your mind right for sobriety!

(BB pg. 24) "At a certain point in the drinking of every alcoholic, he passes into a state where the most powerful desire to stop drinking is of absolutely no avail." There is a solution and it's in the literature and the 12 Steps.

Sun - Living Sober (Will answer many questions newcomers have about sobriety.) For example: Can I go to a party? Can I keep liquor in the house? What about sex? What about drinking dreams?

Mon/Wed - Steps 1, 2, 3 & 10, 11, 12 (Stop doing the 1, 2, 3 shuffle. Start living in Steps 10, 11 & 12 while you do Steps 1 thru 9!)

Tues - Drop the Rock (Find out about our Character Defects. Letting go of what's weighing us down emotionally and mentally.)

Thurs - Big Book Study (We read and discuss the BB line by line to pg. 164.)

Fri - Beginner's Step Meeting (We cover Steps 1 thru 12 in order. A different step each week.)

Sat - Big Book Stories (We listen to and discuss a BB story each week)

Join us every month as we go thru the 12 Steps in 4 hours on Zoom. We also have a 1-hour Recovery Workshop every month. Our other workshops are now on Podcast. 

Come get Virtual Chips and tell us how many days/months/years you have! How about a Zoo Crew Pink Cloud or Rule 62 Chip? Everyone's time is important!

Join the Zoo Crew Service Team. We will train you. E-mail: Zoom247AA@gmail.com

name: AA Daily Meditation Meeting
notes:
Please click on the email link to receive an email with the login information for this meeting.

The AA Daily Meditation Meetings on Zoom are based on the AA Daily Reflections. 
The meetings start with 15 minutes of meditation followed by the reading of the AA Daily Reflection and shares. 
Our meetings are closed. 
Fellows are free to share in English or French since our common language is the language of the heart. 
Our primary purpose is to stay sober and to help other alcoholics to achieve sobriety.

name: Vision for Us 24/7
notes:
WELCOME Vision for Us MEETING Etiquettes:
1. Our singleness of purpose which states that, we ask that all who participate confine your discussion to your problems with alcohol.
2. Each meeting has but one primary purpose - to carry its message to the alcoholic who still suffers.
3. Share on YOUR recovery! Using “I” statements. Follow AA Traditions and read AA literature.
4. Five-minute shares, Chair will minimize their comments between shares.
5. Absolutely NO criticism (INCLUDING IN CHAT).
6. If you have a problem with the meeting, you can find another one at: aa.org
7. If you have a problem with a person in the meeting, do not bring it into the meeting.

**If you would like to share please raise your virtual hand and turn your camera on for verification. You may turn it off after verification.**

name: Dearborn Plus
notes: This is a closed discussion meeting for anyone who has a desire to stop drinking.

name: 9 pm A Check-Up from the Neck Up Beginner's - Get Your Mind Right
notes:
We meet 7 days a week at 9 pm EST for a Topic Discussion. Beginners and Old-Timers are welcome! We make sobriety FUN!

Originated out of the USA but we are International. We are now open 24 hours 7 days a week! The Zoo Crew has been on Zoom since March of 2020. When the world shut down during the pandemic, we opened our Virtual Doors! 

Alcoholism centers in the mind. It is cunning, baffling and powerful and unless the obsession to drink is removed we are defenseless against that first sucker drink. (BB pg. 37) “Our behavior is as absurd and incomprehensible with respect to the first drink as that of an individual with a passion, say, for jay-walking.” Come get your mind right for sobriety!

Come get a Virtual White, Pink Cloud or Rule 62 Chip and tell us how many days/months/years you have! Get a 1-Minute Chip! Everyone's time is important!

name: 100 Men And Women Alcoholics Anonymous Meeting
notes: 100 Men And Women AA meeting is a daily Zoom meeting that follows the AA Steps and Traditions "To show other alcoholics precisely how we have recovered from a seemingly hopeless state of mind and body" is the main purpose of this meeting! Closed Meeting. Topic/Literature

name: 6 am EST Check-up from the Neck Up Early Bird
notes:
We meet 7 days a week at 6 am EST. Get the Early Bird Special with the Original Zoo Crew! Come get your mind right for the day!

The Zoo Crew has been on Zoom since March of 2020. When the world shut down during the pandemic, we opened our Virtual Doors!

We are based in the USA but we are truly international. Our first meeting is at 6 am EST and the last one finishes at midnight with fellowship into the late morning. We are open over 24 hours for all the drinking holidays and the major
holidays. You NEVER have to be alone!

We are going on a Sober Cruise in May of 2023. Join us.

Alcoholism centers in the mind. It is cunning, baffling and powerful and unless the obsession to drink is removed we are defenseless against that first sucker drink. (BB pg. 37) “Our behavior is as absurd and incomprehensible with respect to the first drink as that of an individual with a passion, say, for jay-walking.”

M/T/W - On Awakening & Daily Reflections
Th/F - No Matter What (We don't drink)
Sat/Sun - Check-up from the Neck Up (Get your mind right)

Join us every month as we go thru the 12 Steps in 2 hours on Zoom. Come to our 12 Traditions Workshop every 2 months.

We also have a 1-hour Recovery Workshop every month. Our other workshops are now on Podcast. 

Join the Zoo Crew Service Team. We will train you. E-mail: Zoom247AA@gmail.com

name: Queer & Sober: Monday - Friday
A New Thought, New Choices
notes:
We are an open meeting of Alcoholics Anonymous. All are welcome. 
We offer a loving and safe community focused on RECOVERY using the 12 STEPS and the 12 TRADITIONS.
We do open the meeting 30 minutes prior for fellowship. 
Sun: Speaker Meeting
Mon: Daily Reflection
Tue: Daily Reflection
Wed: Daily Reflection
Thu: Daily Reflections

name: Sisters in Recovery HOW
notes:
AA Zoom Women's Meeting - Well rounded solid AA Group Started in March of 2020 as Sisters in Sobriety NTX
Open Literature meeting

name: Jwala Beginners Malayalam Online AA Group
notes:
Morning Recovery ( രാവിലെ ) 
7 days a week at 6.30 am-8.30 am

name: Breakfast Reflections
notes:
A daily reflections meeting focusing on the day's reading, and supporting each other. 
Open to all. Meets on Friday, Saturday and Sunday at 7am EST for one hour.

name: AA NEJC
notes: Northeast Johnson County Zoom Meetings; 10 AM Central, Tuesdays, Thursdays, Saturdays and Sundays.  Lots of great sobriety attending this meeting.

name: Hampstead Women's Meeting
notes:
This is a women’s closed AA Big Book lead meeting. We meet Sundays at 5pm London UK time on zoom and welcome all women, anywhere in the world, who have a desire to stop drinking. We ask that your video be
on when you enter the room.

name: Pimlico Keep It Simple Online meeting
notes: Pimlico London “Keep it Simple” is a weekly AA zoom meeting with an invited chair/speaker, focusing on “keep it simple”everyone is welcome to attend. The Meeting opens every Tuesday at 12.45 pm starting at 1pm, GMT UK time duration “1hour” In the recovery world, “Keep It Simple” has been a familiar phrase since Alcoholics Anonymous began in 1939. It was the favorite phrase of Dr. Bob, one of the cofounders of AA. During his last speech at the national AA convention in 1950, he spoke of the simplicity of the AA solution.

name: Sisters of Serenity
notes:
Hello and Welcome to SOS!
We, here at SOS, like to offer a safe place for women to continue to recover together. We come from all walks of life and continue to engage in love, patience and tolerance. To another day being sober! 
For more information or gaining access to our meeting room / calendar, please access our website: 
https://lafwomen.wixsite.com/sistersofserenity 
Sisters of Serenity Meetings 
Sunday 8am est (5am pst) On Awakening 
Monday 1pm est (10am pst) Grapevine stories 
Tuesday 6pm est (3pm pst) Emotional Sobriety 
Friday 6pm est (3pm pst) Book study: Voices of Women in AA 
Saturday 8am est (5am pst) On Awakening 
Saturday 11am est (8am pst) Grapevine Stories 
We look forward to connecting with you soon!
~ SOS Trusted Servant 
For attendance verification: 
1. Please have your video on throughout the meeting  
2. During the meeting send the chairperson a message in the chat, informing her that you are looking for verification during the meeting. 
3. Soon after the meeting, send an email to SistersOfSerenitySOS@gmail.com with your name and what meeting attended for verification. One of the admins will respond to your request in a timely manner.
Thank you, we look forward to Trudging the Road of Happy Destiny with you
-Sisters of Serenity-

name: Daily Reflection Coffee Connection
notes:
A Zoom meeting that focuses on reading and discussing from the book Daily Reflections.
We Know Only a Little Meeting Directory

Monday through Friday
Daily Reflections & Coffee, Connection 11am EST

Monday-Discussions of Grapevine 8pm EST
Tuesday- Step Study 9PM EST
Wednesday- Literature 8pm EST
Thursday – Big Book 9pm EST
Friday- Newcomers 8pm EST
Saturday- Saturday Night Live 9PM EST

Per our Group Conscious we do not provide verification.

name: Just for Today Virtual AA
notes:
Daily Virtual AA Meeting (7 days); Hosted via zoom
6 am (45 minutes)
7 am (60 minutes)
- Closed
- Literature Based Discussion
Closed meetings are for A.A. members only, or for those who have a drinking problem and “have a desire to stop drinking.”
In the interest of safety -  send a request for access to our virtual meetings to:  JFTContactList@gmail.com
Your contact information will remain confidential until, and unless ,you choose to opt-in to our group contact list.

name: UK AA Morning Reading / Sharing
notes: At this meeting we usually read the daily reflection and upon awakening. Then we open the meeting for solution based sharing.

name: AA Beginners Meeting: Serenity Seekers
notes:
Join "Serenity Seekers Beginners Meeting" – a warm, inclusive AA gathering for newcomers. 

We embrace all with open arms and offer support to those seeking serenity on their journey to recovery. No one is ever turned away; all that's required is the desire to stop drinking.

Welcome home!

name: The Pickle Jar Women's AA Meetings
notes:
"Once a cucumber becomes a pickle, it can never be a cucumber again!

Proud Pickles gather 7 days a week, 11 am EST and @ 8 pm EST

Meeting formats will vary from Host to Host. Still, they will include Big Book Studies, Daily Reflections, Inspirational Stories, readings from Women in Recovery, and anything else our pickled minds can come up with. We pickles like to color outside the jar!

The opposite of addiction is connection, and with the singleness of purpose in mind, we hope to build a community where all women in recovery feel safe, loved, and supported. We don't do this alone!

Check out our website for meeting access, resources, and a private community page.

name: Exconvento AA
notes: Es una Reunion de apoyo para AA

name: A Checkup From the Neck Up - Music & More Beginner's Meeting
notes:
We meet 7 days a week at 2:30 pm EST. Beginners and old-timers are Welcome! Sobriety is not boring. Get your mind right!

We play music in this meeting and then discuss it. Songs that had a positive impact on your sobriety. Join us for fun and fellowship!

The Zoo Crew has been on Zoom since March of 2020. We have picnics around the country for meet and greets. Come join our community.


Join the Zoo Crew Service Team. Watch our training video: https://youtu.be/5ivUO0PK3j8

name: Shoulder to Shoulder
notes:
Shoulder to Shoulder is a closed meeting of Alcoholics Anonymous. All who have a desire to stop drinking are welcome to join us. We meet online every Sunday at 9:00am ET to study the text of the Big Book of Alcoholics Anonymous to get a better understanding of the ideas, principles and steps of AA. We also like to enjoy ourselves while we do this and, if
we are blessed, make a friend or two!
We read paragraph by paragraph and participants can come in at any time to share. We encourage cross talk which fosters a friendly and emotionally connected meeting. We offer fellowship with understanding, support and humor. AND, free verification of attendance.
Newcomers and Oldtimers alike are welcome!

name: Meeker Group
notes: Daily open meetings at 6AM and 6PM PST

name: Grupo AA La Red
notes:
Tenemos reuniones abiertas de estructuración y paso doce y cerradas de compartires.

Nuestro propósito principal es llevar el mensaje al alcohólico que aun esta sufriendo Trad. 5
Si necesitas ayuda con el tema del alcohol, estamos para ayudarte, comunícate con nosotros al WhatsApp
Teléfono Whatsapp: +57 3142174160
https://chat.whatsapp.com/IkqS1OfyiObLl8SuPGOSMr

name: Out to Breakfast Men's Meeting
notes:
Welcome to our men's closed meeting of Alcoholics Anonymous! 
Our format includes a speaker/topic session, and everyone is encouraged to share. 
We're here to support each other on our journey to recovery.

For safety and anonymity, it is a group conscious decision to ask attendees to turn on their camera, if you have one, and if possible, leave it on for the meeting.

name: InsomniAA
notes:
Join "InsomniAA" – a warm, inclusive meeting of Alcoholics Anonymous for newcomers and old timers alike at 11:30 pm pacific time in the USA every night.
We embrace all with open arms and offer support to those seeking sobriety on their journey to recovery.
No one is ever turned away; all that's required to participate is the desire to stop drinking.
Our primary purpose is to stay sober and help other alcoholics achieve sobriety, and we follow the 12 Traditions of A.A.
Different format each night. Welcome!

name: Into Action Online
notes:
A daily Zoom discussion meeting focusing on the 12 steps of Alcoholics Anonymous. 
It is an open meeting, and all are welcome! 

We meet Monday-Friday 6:30-7:30 am MST
Saturday 8:00-9:00 am MST
Sunday 7:30-8:30 am MST

Tuesday is a step study
Friday is a traditions meeting.

name: 5 O'Clock Sober
notes:
5 O'Clock Sober in an open AA meeting that meets 7-days a week at 5:00 p.m. EST including various topics and formats:

Monday - Daily Reflection Discussion Meeting
Tuesday - Guest Speaker Meeting
Wednesday - Big Book Study (Reading & Discussion)
Thursday - Tag Discussion
Friday - 12 Steps & 12 Traditions (Reading & Discussion)
Saturday - Tag Discussion
Sunday - Emotional Sobriety (Reading & Discussion)

name: Solstice Group AA
notes:
Weekly closed-discussion meeting. Recovery topics from Daily Reflections readings. 
Open to all who have the desire to stop drinking.
Pagan-friendly, LGBTQ-friendly.

name: Bentonville Noon
notes:
Our meetings are literature based focused on the Big Book and the 12&12. We share our experience, strength and hope in an effort to share the message of recovery. 
This is an open meeting held every weekday at 12 to 1PM CST.
Monday - Big Book
Tuesday - 12 & 12
Wednesday - Stories
Thursday - Big Book
Friday - Big Book, Newcomer

name: Oasis Group
notes:
A 4x a week meeting in which everyone can share! 
You do not have to raise hands to be called on. 
Friday is our speaker meeting. The other days are a check in meeting where you may discuss or share anything you wish. 
2 pm PST/ 5 pm EST

name: Mornings With Bill W.
notes:
We meet every day at 7:30 AM Eastern time. We’re glad you’re here! Our current meeting lineup is: Monday- Big Book, Tuesday- As Bill Sees It, Wednesday- speaker, Thursday-step meetings, Friday- Tradition/Grapevine/celebration, Weekends-chairs choice. Wednesday and Friday are open meetings, with all others days closed. 

When you join the meeting, you will be placed in the waiting room. Please be patient as our greeter is managing the meeting safety. Be prepared to turn on your camera as soon as you enter the meeting. If your camera is not working, we suggest using the phone  option listed above. Hope to see you soon!

name: Grupo Nuevo Renacer, de AA de Cienaga (Magdalena)/Colombia
notes: Necesita una reunión? Vengase para acá! lo estamos esperando! Mente Abierta, mente alerta.

name: Alcoholics Anonymous Recovery with Challenges (AARC)
notes:
Alcoholics Anonymous Recovery with Challenges (AARC)  is dedicated to providing a welcoming and supportive environment for individuals recovering from alcoholism who also face challenges related to mental and physical health, including trauma and chronic conditions.
We meet every Sunday at 3: 00 EST, Tuesday at 8 pm EST, Wednesday at 3 PM EST and Thursday at 9:00 PM EST on Zoom to listen to a speaker share their own story or read a story from AA-approved literature that focuses on our needs. Relevant topics are discussed during the sharing portion of the meeting.

name: Grupo No Estamos Solos de AA
notes:
Nos reunimos por ZOOM. Abierto a compañeros/as alcohólicos/as de todo el mundo.
Reunión TODOS LOS DÍAS DE LA SEMANA a las 7.00 am España /22:00 Obregón (México) Duración: una hora y media. En ESPAÑOL.

El funcionamiento del grupo es en base al seguimiento y respeto de las DOCE TRADICIONES.
Tenemos una programación semanal, según la cual, cada día tratamos un tema: Conceptos, Tradiciones, Pasos o temas de la literatura de AA.
Participaciones de 10 minutos. Reuniones especiales los domingos con un orador.

name: Everyday Living
notes:
Meets every Tuesday, Thursday, and Friday 12:00 - 1:00 EST
Focus is on reading a portion of the 12 Steps and 12 Traditions, followed by discussion.
All are welcome to attend.
No password needed.

name: Friday Night Live Every Night
notes:
FNLEN is here every night 8pm EST.
Monday - Big Book study
Tuesday - open discussion
Wednesday - 12 & 12 study
Thursday - speaker night
Friday - As Bill Sees It
Saturday - open discussion
Sunday - open discussion
Stick with the winners, keep coming back

name: AA Aussie Worldwide
notes:
Sun Spiritual Concepts
Mon. Big Book
Tues Beginners
Wed Living Sober
Wed Living Sober
Thurs Daily Reflections
Fri ID
Sat Topic

name: Sunrise Online
notes: The Sunrise Online Group is an online, literature-based, discussion meeting that offers daily-changing topics and references a multitude of AA-approved literature. We are a group of recovering alcoholics that understand and maintain the integrity of the 12 traditions. We have no outside affiliation and our primary purpose is to stay sober and help other alcoholics achieve sobriety.

name: Turn The Page
notes:
Solution focused.
Based out of Knoxville TN
Seven days a week at 6 PM EST
Monday: Open Topic
Tuesday: Big Book Speaker
Wednesday: Big Book Cards
Thursday: Open Topic
Friday:  - Open Chair
Saturday: Dealers Choice
Sunday: Big Book Stories

name: Step Sisters
notes:
Open Women’s meeting  Sunday thru Thursday at 7pm PST.
Sunday is a Big Book Study.
Thursday is a Twelve Steps and Twelve Traditions Study.
Saturday will be a Living Sober night.
 
One hour,  Sunday thru Thursday at 7pm PST.

name: Iran Mornings woman
notes: A daily virtual telephone meeting held from 10:00 AM to 11:30 AM Tehran time (IRST, UTC +3:30), every Monday through Thursday. We follow the AA Big Book sequentially, reading one section at a time and discussing its meaning together. The meeting is open to all—whether you’re new to the program or have long been in recovery. Sessions last 90 minutes and include group reading turns, thoughtful reflections, personal sharing, and warm fellowship. No prior registration is required. Anonymity is respected, and newcomers are always welcomed. Join us to deepen your understanding and strengthen your recovery journey.

name: Pacific Northwest Fellowship
notes:
PNWF is a fellowship rooted firmly in the Big Book of Alcoholics Anonymous and guided by the Twelve Traditions. This is a space where newcomers and old-timers alike can come together in unity, service, and recovery. 

Our primary purpose is to stay sober and help other alcoholics achieve sobriety, just as outlined in the program of Alcoholics Anonymous. We read directly from the Big Book, share personal experience, strength, and hope, and keep our group grounded in the spiritual principles that have helped millions find freedom from alcoholism.

Though we meet online, we’re not just a digital group — we encourage members to connect locally as well.

name: The Unfortunates Group
notes: Open Discussion Mon-Sun 7am CST

name: Chelsea Nooners Remote
notes:
A twice-weekly video meeting with a speaker followed by sharing. 
This is a closed meeting. If you are an alcoholic, or have a desire to stop drinking, you are welcome to attend. 
Meets every Monday and Wednesday for 1 hour from 12pm till 1pm EST.

name: GRUPO CENTINEL ONLINE
notes:
Somos un grupo online de Chile que se reúne todos los días al mediodía para llevar el mensaje al alcohólico que aún sufre. Además, contamos con dos reuniones semanales en horario nocturno.
Extendemos una cordial invitación a todas las personas que deseen unirse a nuestras reuniones. Lunes a domingos a las 13:00 Horas de Chile
Martes y Jueves a las 21:00 Horas de Chile

🔺CENTINELA ONLINE🔺
Toda la información actualizada se encuentra en:
https://www.centinelaonline.cl

name: A New Beginning (UK based)
notes:
We meet on a Monday, Wednesday, Friday and Sunday (12noon to 1pm UK time) and Tuesday (7pm to 8pm UK time). An AA Open meeting - all welcome. Each meeting has a theme - (room opens 15 minutes prior for fellowship)

 Sunday is  A New Beginning Experience, Strength and Hope
 Monday is Daily Reflections
 Tuesday is Keep it Simple;
 Wednesday is  As Bill Sees It 
 Friday is Living Sober.

name: K.I.S.S. ( Keep It Simply Spiritual ) A.A. GROUP
notes:
Our commitment is: • Solutions offered • Spiritually purposeful • Literature A.A. driven • Praying:  Recognizing that there is something bigger than ourselves

This SOLUTION focused group meets 7 days per week - 2x daily. • 12 noon (12pm ) EASTERN USA • 8:15pm EASTERN

All meetings are Open Discussion

• SUNDAY– 12pm EASTERN - Emotional Sobriety
• MONDAY - SATURDAY - 12pm EASTERN – Live in the Solution
• SUNDAY - 8:15pm EASTERN – Guest Speaker
• MONDAY - 8:15pm EASTERN – Literature (Big Book, 12&12, etc)
• TUESDAY - 8:15pm EASTERN – Hot Topic Meeting
• WEDNESDAY - 8:15pm EASTERN – Newcomer’s Meeting
• THURSDAY - 8:15pm EASTERN – Old-Timer’s Meeting
• FRIDAY - 8:15pm EASTERN - Women's Meeting (EMAIL US FOR THE PASSCODE)
• SATURDAY - 8:15pm EASTERN – Literature Night

name: AA The Way Out
notes:
Co-Ed Meeting's daily @ 6am EST
Women's Meeting's @ 10am & 6:15pm EST daily
Men's Meeting 8pm EST on Sunday's

AA The Way Out is a welcoming and solution-focused group dedicated to providing a safe and supportive environment for men and women seeking recovery. We offer a variety of meetings to accommodate different needs and schedules. Our daily coed meeting is held at 6:00 AM, providing early morning options for connection and support. Additionally, we host a women's meeting at 10:00 AM EST and another at 6:15 PM EST, creating a focused space for women in recovery.  A new Sunday 8PM EST meeting for Men has been created for men in recovery.

Our group is a place to call home, where members find strength, understanding, and hope as they walk the path of sobriety together. Through shared experience, spiritual growth, and daily connection, we offer consistent meetings that support healing, personal growth, and community. Alcoholism affects us all, but by sharing our experiences and supporting one another, we find hope and a common solution.

Meeting Schedule:
6:00 AM Coed 
10:00 AM Women 
6:15 PM Women

10:00 AM Women's EST Meetings:
    Monday: Stories of AA
    Tuesday: Ask-It-Basket
    Wednesday: Prayer & Meditation
    Thursday: AA Literature
    Friday: Step Meeting
    Saturday: Big Book Study
    Sunday: Super Speaker Sunday

6:15 PM Women's EST. Meetings
    Monday - Wednesday - Daily Reflection
    Thursday - AA Literature
    Friday - Music Meeting
    Saturday - Music Meeting
    Sunday - Super Speaker

name: Unity and Belonging
notes:
Unity & Belonging is an open online AA meeting focused on the LGBTQ+ community and our allies. All alcoholics are welcome! Meetings are held daily at 5pm Eastern. The primary purpose of the group Unity & Belonging is to follow the guideline of Tradition 5. We do this by providing a supportive space for individuals seeking recovery from alcoholism, adhering to the AA Principles, and ensuring the AA traditions guide the decisions of the group.
Sunday: Big Book Reading/ Discussion
Monday: Living Sober Reading/ Discussion
Tuesday: AA Grapevine Reading/ Discussion
Wednesday: Meditation/Daily Reflection/ Discussion
Thursday: Chair’s Choice
Friday: Speaker Traditions meeting/ Discussion
Saturday: Speaker Step meeting/ Discussion

name: Riverdale Group Online
notes:
Thursdays at 6:30-7:30 PM. Big Book Meeting.
Members read Big Book cover-to-cover and share on the reading, (round robin)

name: WISH Group of Alcoholics Anonymous
notes:
WISH - Women In Service Here
A women's group of Alcoholics Anonymous where we celebrate women in recovery through fellowship, service and recovery.

We host a variety of meetings each day, some of which change every three-six months and hold a monthly Group Conscience meeting.

Our regular meetings are as follows (all times are EST)...

6am On Awakening - Daily
7am Living Sober - Sundays
10am Living Sober - Mondays
6pm Big Book Study - Tuesdays & Saturdays
6pm 12&12 - Thursdays
7pm AA Literature - Sundays & Wednesdays

The Parking Lot is open after most meetings for fellowship and Rule 62!

Many more meetings available via our weekly schedule which is provided
at the meeting.

name: AA FELLOWSHIP AT SUNSET SERENITY
notes:
We are an open AA fellowship meeting 2 times daily. Weather you have few days or many years in sobriety, we invite you to join us in recovery, unity, and service. We also encourage discussions about mental and physical health affecting alcoholism and addiction. 
☆" ONCE YOU FIND US YOU NEVER LEAVE US "☆ 
Monday: Gratitude Day
Tuesday: Open Topic Day
Wednesday:  : Big Book study/Share
Thursday: Daily Reflection
Friday: Music & Share
Sat: Speaker / Topic Share
Sun: Speaker / bring a friend
* Court Card V Available *
♡ 12 Pacific (3 Eastern) / 6 Pacific Daily (9 Eastern)  ♡

name: Grupo el milagro
notes:
Grupo el milagro es un grupo tradicional que estudie la literatura de AA. Abierta a todos; todo el mundo es bienvenido. Hay reuniones todos los días a las 12PM hora Miami (EST) y la duración es una hora. Papeles de la corte disponible. 
Lunes: Estudio Libro Azul
Martes: Serie de tradiciones
Miércoles: Libro Viviendo Sobrio
Jueves: Orador
Viernes: Libro, Como Lo Ve Bill
Sábado: Reunión de temas
Domingo: Serie de Pasos
Por razones de seguridad, la conciencia del group acordó que por favor entren con sus cámaras abiertas.

name: A.A. KASTORIA
notes:
Είμαστε μια διαδικτυακή ομάδα που επικεντρώνεται στην ανάγνωση και τα μοιράσματα πάνω στην βιβλιογραφία των Α.Α. 
Είμαστε ανοιχτοί σε όλους· όλοι είναι ευπρόσδεκτοι να συμμετάσχουν. 
Συναντιόμαστε στις 8 μ.μ. (ώρα Ανατολικής Ευρώπης) 4 ημέρες την εβδομάδα. 
Το τελευταίο Σάββατο κάθε μήνα, η συνάντηση είναι ανοιχτή σε μη μέλη των Α.Α.
